title: Officer Pastorello and Maria Holmes Compassionate Policing Resolution
summary: Councilor Moore presents resolution honoring Syracuse Police Officer Jamie Pastorello for helping Maria Holmes, a 55-year-old woman sleeping on her late husband's grave at Oakwood Cemetery. Pastorello secured immediate shelter, launched a GoFundMe, and connected Holmes with Tiny Home For Good and Le Moyne College for permanent housing. Moore also shares examples of community policing: officers comforting a grieving widow, Officer Thorrett investigating an illegal eviction saving residents from freezing, and Officer Wheeling escorting a truant student back to school. Police Chief Mark Russen's letter of support read. National news story. Passes unanimously.

political_signal.description: Councilor Moore champions compassionate policing narrative with multiple examples of officer community engagement, reinforcing positive SPD public image

processing_notes: National news story. Officer name is Pastorello (variously transcribed as Pastrol, Pastelli, Pasterelli, Pastor Bell). Detected departments SFD, DIT, Law Dept are false positives. Agenda item 28 (Pi Kappa Epsilon SFD behavioral health donation) was voted at top of segment; bulk of discussion is for item 29 (Pastorello resolution).
Segment 5 of 10 from Common Council Regular Meeting, Monday February 23rd 2026
